body: This change applies to the following licences: Temporary Expanded Service Area (TESA) authorizationswill extend toDecember 31, 2024.The new expiry date be reflected insection 109.5 of the Liquor Control and Licensing Regulation. Local governments and First Nationshave until March 20, 2023to raise concerns about TESAs in their jurisdiction. Licensing staff workwith local authorities and licensees to resolve concerns about TESAs. If the local authorities have no concerns, the TESA authorizations will extend toDecember 31, 2024.The Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch (LCRB) will reissue authorization letters with the new expiry date.Licensees do not need to apply for extension and there is no cost. Find outhow to make a TESA permanent. Bulletin 23-01 replacespolicy directive 22-05and the previous expiry date of March 31, 2023. Bulletins are for general information and may not apply to all situations. Bulletins do not constitute legal advice nor are they a comprehensive statement of the legal obligations that arise under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act, Cannabis Control and Licensing Act, regulations, or any other applicable laws. When interpreting and applying the information contained in the Bulletins, you are encouraged to seek advice from your professional advisors as appropriate in the circumstances. The B.C. Public Service acknowledges the territories of First Nations around B.C. and is grateful to carry out our work on these lands. We acknowledge the rights, interests, priorities, and concerns of all Indigenous Peoples - First Nations, Métis, and Inuit - respecting and acknowledging their distinct cultures, histories, rights, laws, and governments.

minutes_briefs.title: TESA authorizations extended to December 31, 2024
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has extended Temporary Expanded Service Area (TESA) authorizations — permits that let liquor licensees serve customers in a larger area than usual — until December 31, 2024. The previous expiry date was March 31, 2023. Licensees do not need to apply for the extension and there is no cost. Local governments and First Nations had until March 20, 2023 to raise concerns, but that date has already passed.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No action needed — your TESA authorization is automatically extended to December 31, 2024.

key_dates.label: TESA authorization extension takes effect (new expiry date)
